content.full_text:
May 3, 1990

Kathryn E. Donovan, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

RE: 2 Cal Code of Regs. Section 18535, Section C-2

Dear Ms. Donovan:

On April 27, 1990 I spoke with Ms. Blanca Breeze of the Legal Division of the FPPC regarding the above regulation. She suggested that I request written advice regarding the following scenario:

A candidate or his representative attends a luncheon meeting of a county political organization. The cost of the luncheon ticket is $15. Because candidates are prohibited from using campaign funds to make contributions (including the purchase of tickets to political fundraisers) to other candidates or elected officers or to committees which support or oppose candidates, is it permissible for the candidate's campaign to pay for the luncheon ticket? Also, is the fact that the amount would presumably cover the cost of the food alone significant?

We appreciate your assistance in helping unravel the mysteries of the new guidelines.

Sincerely,

(Mrs.) Diane Luenenberg
Assistant Treasurer
Friends of Chris Chandler 1990
335 Teegarden Street
Yuba City, CA 95991
